channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: S&P 500 falls on global chip rout with Nasdaq off more than 1%, led by Micron: Live updates
item.description: The S&P 500 was lower on Tuesday as a tech sell-off that began during the prior session picked up steam overnight.

item.title: Factory job cuts in June neared financial crisis and Covid levels, S&P says
item.description: Though the firm's manufacturing index ran better than expected for June, it came largely from an inventory rebuild and despite sharp job cuts.

item.title: Meta announces new smart glasses starting at $299, as Zuckerberg keeps pushing wearables
item.description: Meta executives have said they see the lightweight smart glasses as a step towards a more advanced device that includes screens in the lenses.

item.title: Could there be a $10 trillion company? Tech investing star Laffont believes so.
item.description: A new regime of trillion-dollar companies is replacing the 'Magnificent Seven.'

item.title: SpaceX stock climbs 4% after falling below $150 debut price
item.description: Gains have been pared back at the space and AI company following an initial surge off of its record-breaking IPO.

item.title: Costco could be a winner from the big market rotation. How to trade it
item.description: Nishant Pant breaks down how he's trading COST.

item.title: U.S. issues sweeping Iran oil sanctions waivers, unlocking billions in revenue for Tehran
item.description: The U.S. issued a sweeping rollback of sanctions on Iranian oil, offering Tehran an economic lifeline as negotiations toward a peace deal proceed.

item.title: Sen. Cassidy plans to push 'big idea' for Social Security reform in last days in office
item.description: As Social Security faces an imminent funding shortfall, Sen. Bill Cassidy said he has come up with a plan to invest in the stock market on the program's behalf.

item.title: 'I like their money': Trump threatens lawsuits against ABC for reporting on Reflecting Pool
item.description: The latest action against ABC comes as the broadcaster faces two investigations from the Federal Communications Commission.

item.title: Trump administration to loan $17 billion to speed deployment of 10 big nuclear reactors in U.S.
item.description: The loans will support the construction of five projects that each host two big nuclear reactors.

item.title: Five things to watch in Tuesday's primaries as New York races take center stage
item.description: Tuesday’s primaries in New York, Maryland and Utah test outside money, party power and the influence of Zohran Mamdani and Donald Trump.

item.title: AI PACs pour $20 million into New York Democratic primary in AI regulation battle
item.description: Companies are spending big to shape AI policies at the federal level. And the Manhattan House Democratic primary will be a test of where voters stand.

item.title: Progressive Brad Lander’s surging bid for Congress splinters Democrats' labor union alliance
item.description: Rep. Dan Goldman, D-N.Y., is fighting for his political life in a race with Brad Lander, who is backed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani and Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt.

item.title: Brexit 10 years later: How the UK economy and politics changed, in charts
item.description: Ten years after the Brexit vote, CNBC compiled these charts show how the U.K. has changed across growth, immigration, sterling, trade and politics.

item.title: Oracle sheds 21,000 roles over the past year amid wave of AI layoffs from tech giants
item.description: "The adoption and deployment of AI technologies across our operations have resulted, and may continue to result, in reductions to our workforce," Oracle said.

item.title: One factor may keep chip stocks on a roller coaster for foreseeable future
item.description: Tech is dragging global stock markets lower Tuesday, with the sell-off driven primarily by chipmakers. Leveraged ETFs could stoke the volatility.

item.title: Gold and silver tumble as rate-hike fears hit precious metals
item.description: Gold and silver fell Tuesday as rate-hike fears pressured precious metals and Wall Street banks reassessed their gold forecasts.

item.title: Sens. Warren, Kelly press Trump administration on effects of tariffs on manufacturing
item.description: A pair of Democratic senators is asking for answers from the Trump administration on the effect of tariffs on U.S. manufacturing.

item.title: AI memory startup focused on cutting token costs raises $98 million
item.description: Engram enters the market as the AI industry grapples with a rising cost problem fueled by more expensive models.

item.title: Apollo curbs withdrawals after exit requests hit 17%, reigniting fears over private credit liquidity
item.description: Apollo is capping investor redemptions in its main retail-focused private credit fund after exit requests hit 17%.

item.title: We're adding to our position in an industrial giant with a major catalyst nearing
item.description: We want to own more of the stock ahead of its long-awaited breakup.

item.title: Prime Day shoppers: Should you pick the Chase Sapphire Preferred over the Prime Visa?
item.description: The Chase Sapphire Preferred's welcome bonus is 5X more valuable than the Prime Visa's, but there are reasons to have both.

item.title: Trump Account signups now total more than 6 million, but millions more children are eligible
item.description: Trump Accounts will soon be available to millions of U.S. children; however, many may miss out on the savings opportunity — and free money.

item.title: Tesla faces federal probe after Model 3 slams into Texas home, killing 76-year-old
item.description: Harris County authorities said that the driver, Michael Butler, said that he had been using Tesla's partially automated driving systems.

item.title: Highly successful people do 5 things to get luckier in life, says Stanford leadership expert: 'Good luck isn't random or rare'
item.description: Stanford University educator Tina Seelig shares what the luckiest people do every day to find success.

item.title: How Europe is grappling with extreme heat as red alerts issued in Britain, France, Spain and Italy
item.description: Several European countries issued red weather alerts as a fresh bout of extreme heat pushed temperatures beyond 40 degrees Celsius (104 degrees Fahrenheit).

item.title: U.S. fights with Brazil for China's giant soybean market
item.description: The U.S. Soybean Export Council makes claims to higher quality a selling point over Brazil's crop, as American farmers attempt to win back business from the world's largest importer

item.title: Lucid to lay off roughly 18% of U.S. workforce, COO Marc Winterhoff leaves
item.description: Lucid Group is cutting its U.S. workforce by 18% as part of a cost-savings plan. It also said COO Marc Winterhoff is departing effective immediately.

item.title: Ro Khanna challenges Elon Musk to televised debate after online DOGE battle
item.description: Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., has been a critic of the Elon-Musk-led Department of Government Efficiency cuts.

item.title: How a $4 billion Indian startup won Meta's backing but lost its founder to WhatsApp
item.description: Indian fintech startup Cred will raise $900 million in a round led by Meta, but has lost its founder and CEO to WhatsApp.
